At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:suchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqq]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:suchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]]

Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:suchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "江戸幕府" to "Tokugawa shogunate"
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:suchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:11]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842年}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year }}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year }}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year }} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year }}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year }}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year }}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year }} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year }} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year }}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year }}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year }}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:Nobles of After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|12=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:s11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日|12=draft}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区|12=draft}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|12=draft}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|12=draft}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|12=draft}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year|12=draft}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=draft}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|12=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日|12=draft|12=draft}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区|12=draft|12=draft}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}} | court_rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs|12=draft|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|12=draft|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|12=draft|12=draft}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|12=draft|12=draft}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft|12=draft}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}. His court rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, upon coming of age, he held the rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=draft|12=draft}} and reached the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|12=draft|12=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日|12=draft|12=draft}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区|12=draft|12=draft}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs|12=draft|12=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|12=draft|12=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|12=draft|12=draft}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|12=draft|12=draft}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft|12=draft}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=draft|12=draft}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|12=draft|12=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=draft|12=draft}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|12=draft|12=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|1=Lunisolar calendar|2=ja|3=太陰太陽暦|comment=note that this is not the Japanese lunisolar calendar it is any lunisolar calendar|4=ru|5=Лунно-солнечный календарь|6=de|7=Lunisolarkalender|8=es|9=Calendario lunisolar|10=fr|11=Calendrier luni-solaire|12=draft|12=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft|12=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|1=Lunisolar calendar|2=ja|3=太陰太陽暦|comment=note that this is not the Japanese lunisolar calendar it is any lunisolar calendar|4=ru|5=Лунно-солнечный календарь|6=de|7=Lunisolarkalender|8=es|9=Calendario lunisolar|10=fr|11=Calendrier luni-solaire|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}}

{{出典の明記|date=2015年8月}} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 土御門 晴雄 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[江戸時代]]後期 | 生誕 = [[文政]]10年[[6月5日 (旧暦)|6月5日]]（[[1827年]][[6月28日]]） | 死没 = [[明治]]2年[[10月6日 (旧暦)|10月6日]]（[[1869年]][[11月9日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[京都市]][[下京区]]梅小路[[梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|梅林寺]] | 官位 = [[正三位]]・[[民部省|民部卿]] | 主君 = [[仁孝天皇]]→[[孝明天皇]]→[[明治天皇]] | 氏族 = [[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]] | 父母 = 父：[[土御門晴親]] | 兄弟 = '''晴雄'''、女子（櫛笥隆韶室）、女子、脩子、[[土御門藤子|藤子]]『土御門家譜』には「邦子」とある | 妻 =廣橋三成女 | 子 = 女子
女子
晴綱
男子
'''[[土御門晴榮|晴榮]]'''（養子）
益子（[[土御門晴榮]]室） | 特記事項 = }} '''土御門 晴雄'''（つちみかど はれたけ若杉家文書『土御門晴雄占文案』（京都府立京都学・歴彩館 蔵）に「はれ雄」と署名あり。また、同文書『土御門晴雄訓書』（同館 蔵）に「晴雄」（「雄」に「タケ」の傍訓）とあり。）は、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[公卿]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[土御門晴親]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正三位]]・[[民部省|民部卿]]。[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]][[陰陽道]]の事実上の最後の当主。家禄は183石6斗。 == 経歴 == [[文政]]10年（[[1827年]]）6月5日（文政12年（[[1829年]]）6月5日午刻とも）、土御門晴親と[[家女房]]・圭順院との間に誕生。[[天保]]4年（[[1833年]]）10月18日に[[従五位|従五位上]]に叙されて、同10年（[[1839年]]）に[[元服]]して従五位上・[[大膳職|大膳大夫]]。同13年（[[1842年]]）に陰陽頭となる。[[嘉永]]2年（[[1849年]]）に[[兵衛府|右兵衛佐]]を兼ねる。[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）には正四位下となり、安政5年（[[1858年]]）の[[廷臣八十八卿列参事件]]に参加する。同年12月1日に行われた[[江戸幕府]]14代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[徳川家茂]]の就任式に際して[[侍従]][[高倉永祜]]と共に[[勅使]]として[[江戸城]]に派遣されている。翌年の3月には[[従三位]]、8月には[[民部省|民部卿]]に転じて、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）には正三位となった。[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）民部卿を辞任。 [[明治維新]]によって江戸幕府が崩壊すると、新政府に働きかけて旧幕府の[[天文方]]を廃止に追い込んで、編暦・頒暦といった[[暦]]の権限のみならず、[[測量]]・[[天文現象|天文]]などの管轄権を[[陰陽寮]]が掌握する事に成功する。当時の新政府の中においては、[[富国強兵]]や[[殖産興業]]に直接繋がらないとみなされた[[天文学]]や[[暦法]]に関する関心が極端に低かったのである。更に[[洋学者]]の間で高まりつつあった[[太陽暦]]導入に反対して、[[天保暦]]を改暦して[[太陰太陽暦]]の継続を図るように提案したものの、今度は逆に新政府の関心の低さが災いして、改暦は見送られる事になった。晴雄はなおも改暦を要求したが、病に倒れ43歳で[[薨去]]。法号は楊光院殿正三位安倍晴雄卿。墓所は京都梅小路[[梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|梅林寺]]にある。 晴雄の実子・晴綱が[[慶応]]4年（1868年）に死去していたため、[[錦織家]]からの養子である和丸（後の[[土御門晴榮]]）が後を継いだ。和丸はまだ幼く、更に新政府にも天文や測量は科学の礎でありまた陸海軍の円滑な運営にも欠かせないという正確な認識が広まると共に、それらが古い陰陽寮に縛られる事への危惧や、非科学的な陰陽道が日本の近代科学導入の障害になる事が指摘されるようになり、新政府は晴雄の死の翌年の明治3年（1870年）、陰陽寮を解体した。また暦法は、明治5年（1872年）末に太陽暦である[[グレゴリオ暦]]に移行された。 == 系譜土御門晴榮『土御門家譜』（東京大学史料編纂所蔵） == *父：[[土御門晴親]] *母：圭順院（家女房） *妻：廣橋三成女 **男子：土御門晴綱 *生母不明の子女 **女子（早逝） **女子（早逝） **男子（早逝） **土御門益子（土御門晴榮室） *養子 **男子：土御門晴榮 - [[錦織久隆]]の次男 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つちみかと はるお}} [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)|はるお]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:山城国の人物]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:1827年生]] [[Category:1869年没]]

'''[[土御門晴榮|晴榮]]'''（養子）
益子（[[土御門晴榮]]室） | 特記事項 = }} '''土御門 晴雄'''（つちみかど はれたけ若杉家文書『土御門晴雄占文案』（京都府立京都学・歴彩館 蔵）に「はれ雄」と署名あり。また、同文書『土御門晴雄訓書』（同館 蔵）に「晴雄」（「雄」に「タケ」の傍訓）とあり。）は、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[公卿]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[土御門晴親]]の子。[[官位]]は[[正三位]]・[[民部省|民部卿]]。[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]][[陰陽道]]の事実上の最後の当主。家禄は183石6斗。 == 経歴 == [[文政]]10年（[[1827年]]）6月5日（文政12年（[[1829年]]）6月5日午刻とも）、土御門晴親と[[家女房]]・圭順院との間に誕生。[[天保]]4年（[[1833年]]）10月18日に[[従五位|従五位上]]に叙されて、同10年（[[1839年]]）に[[元服]]して従五位上・[[大膳職|大膳大夫]]。同13年（[[1842年]]）に陰陽頭となる。[[嘉永]]2年（[[1849年]]）に[[兵衛府|右兵衛佐]]を兼ねる。[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）には正四位下となり、安政5年（[[1858年]]）の[[廷臣八十八卿列参事件]]に参加する。同年12月1日に行われた[[江戸幕府]]14代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[徳川家茂]]の就任式に際して[[侍従]][[高倉永祜]]と共に[[勅使]]として[[江戸城]]に派遣されている。翌年の3月には[[従三位]]、8月には[[民部省|民部卿]]に転じて、[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）には正三位となった。[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）民部卿を辞任。 [[明治維新]]によって江戸幕府が崩壊すると、新政府に働きかけて旧幕府の[[天文方]]を廃止に追い込んで、編暦・頒暦といった[[暦]]の権限のみならず、[[測量]]・[[天文現象|天文]]などの管轄権を[[陰陽寮]]が掌握する事に成功する。当時の新政府の中においては、[[富国強兵]]や[[殖産興業]]に直接繋がらないとみなされた[[天文学]]や[[暦法]]に関する関心が極端に低かったのである。更に[[洋学者]]の間で高まりつつあった[[太陽暦]]導入に反対して、[[天保暦]]を改暦して[[太陰太陽暦]]の継続を図るように提案したものの、今度は逆に新政府の関心の低さが災いして、改暦は見送られる事になった。晴雄はなおも改暦を要求したが、病に倒れ43歳で[[薨去]]。法号は楊光院殿正三位安倍晴雄卿。墓所は京都梅小路[[梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|梅林寺]]にある。 晴雄の実子・晴綱が[[慶応]]4年（1868年）に死去していたため、[[錦織家]]からの養子である和丸（後の[[土御門晴榮]]）が後を継いだ。和丸はまだ幼く、更に新政府にも天文や測量は科学の礎でありまた陸海軍の円滑な運営にも欠かせないという正確な認識が広まると共に、それらが古い陰陽寮に縛られる事への危惧や、非科学的な陰陽道が日本の近代科学導入の障害になる事が指摘されるようになり、新政府は晴雄の死の翌年の明治3年（1870年）、陰陽寮を解体した。また暦法は、明治5年（1872年）末に太陽暦である[[グレゴリオ暦]]に移行された。 == 系譜土御門晴榮『土御門家譜』（東京大学史料編纂所蔵） == *父：[[土御門晴親]] *母：圭順院（家女房） *妻：廣橋三成女 **男子：土御門晴綱 *生母不明の子女 **女子（早逝） **女子（早逝） **男子（早逝） **土御門益子（土御門晴榮室） *養子 **男子：土御門晴榮 - [[錦織久隆]]の次男 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === === 出典 === {{reflist}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つちみかと はるお}} [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)|はるお]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:山城国の人物]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:1827年生]] [[Category:1869年没]]

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | birth_date = {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | death_date = {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|November 9|ja|11月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shimogyo Ward|ja|下京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部省|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|ja|仁孝天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|ja|6月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|1827|ja|1827th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in {{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1842|ja|1842nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Tenmonkata|ja|天文方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|1=Lunisolar calendar|2=ja|3=太陰太陽暦|comment=note that this is not the Japanese lunisolar calendar it is any lunisolar calendar|4=ru|5=Лунно-солнечный календарь|6=de|7=Lunisolarkalender|8=es|9=Calendario lunisolar|10=fr|11=Calendrier luni-solaire|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Children: - Sons: Harutsuna - Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:People from Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchimikado Haruo}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}}
