revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 5th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1827|1827]] [[:en:June 28|June 28]]) | death_date = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th month, 6th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:November 9|November 9]]) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyo Ward]] Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}; [[:en:Ministry of Popular Affairs|Ministry of Popular Affairs]] | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] → [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|qq=}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|qq=}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[en:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}, the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1870年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1870年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}, the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Practitioners of Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 5th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1827|1827]] [[:en:June 28|June 28]]) | death_date = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th month, 6th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:November 9|November 9]]) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyo Ward]] Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}; [[:en:Ministry of Popular Affairs|Ministry of Popular Affairs]] | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] → [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|qq=}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1870年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}, the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tsuchimikado family]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 5th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1827|1827]] [[:en:June 28|June 28]]) | death_date = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th month, 6th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:November 9|November 9]]) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyo Ward]] Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}; [[:en:Ministry of Popular Affairs|Ministry of Popular Affairs]] | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] → [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|qq=}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by {{ill|1870|ja|明治3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1870年}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}, the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:土御門家 (安倍氏)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:従三位受位者]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:廷臣八十八卿]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 5th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1827|1827]] [[:en:June 28|June 28]]) | death_date = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th month, 6th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:November 9|November 9]]) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyo Ward]] Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}; [[:en:Ministry of Popular Affairs|Ministry of Popular Affairs]] | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] → [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|qq=}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:江戸時代の公家]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11423480]] Wikidata name: Tsuchimikado Haretake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=August 2015}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Tsuchimikado Harutake (土御門 晴雄) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10, {{ill|6th month, 5th day|ja|6月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th month, 5th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1827|1827]] [[:en:June 28|June 28]]) | death_date = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2, {{ill|10th month, 6th day|ja|10月6日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th month, 6th day|qq=}} ([[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:November 9|November 9]]) | renames = | aliases = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyo Ward]] Ume-no-Koji, {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}} | court_Rank = {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}; [[:en:Ministry of Popular Affairs|Ministry of Popular Affairs]] | allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] → [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] → [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|qq=}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = Daughters
Sons
{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=|1=Minister of Popular Affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishikori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Onmyōji]]

Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=|1=Minister of Popular Affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishikori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]]}} [[sn:Tsuchimikado Haruo]] [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[sn:suchimikado Haruo]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11423480}}

In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=|1=Minister of Popular Affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishikori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11423480}}

His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|qq=|1=Minister of Popular Affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on [[:en:June 28|June 28]], [[:en:1827|1827]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in [[:en:1839|1839]], upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In [[:en:1842|1842]], he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In [[:en:1868|1868]], he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s [[:en:Tenmongata|Tenmonkata]], consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in [[:en:1869|1869]]. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|qq=|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in [[:en:1868|1868]], the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by [[:en:1870|1870]], the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By [[:en:1873|1873]], the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|qq=|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|qq=|1=Nishikori Hisataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:土御門晴雄]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11423480}}
