During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:土御門晴雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11423480}} [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs|en|Ministry of Popular Affairs|WD=Q4388365|lt=Ministry of Popular Affairs|1=Ministry of Popular Affairs}} | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō|1=Emperor Ninkō}} → {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|WD=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei|1=Emperor Kōmei}} → {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} | family = {{ill|Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|ja|土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family (Abe clan)|1=Tsuchimikado clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423478}} | father = {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} | siblings = Harutake (himself), daughters (married {{ill|Kusukake Takayuki|ja|櫛笥隆韶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusukake Takayuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), Shuuko, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Fujiko|ja|土御門藤子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Fujiko|1=Q11423492|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423492}}The Tsuchimikado family records note "Kuniko." | spouse = Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = Daughters

{{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (adopted)
Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) | notable_points = }} '''Tsuchimikado Harutake''' (土御門 晴雄, Tsuchimikado Harutake)The Wakatsugi Family Documents contain a signature "晴雄" with the reading "タケ" for "雄" annotated. Another document bears the name "はれ雄." was a noble of the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was the de facto final head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} and the head of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|1=Q11547969|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|en|June 28|WD=Q2660|lt=June 28|1=June 28}}, {{ill|1827|en|1827|WD=Q7008|lt=1827|1=1827}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839|1=1839}}, upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In {{ill|1842|en|1842|WD=Q7625|lt=1842|1=1842}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:土御門晴雄]] {{wikidata link|Q11423480}} [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

His court Rank was {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Minister of Popular Affairs|ja|民部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Popular Affairs|1=Q11547969|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547969}}. == Career == Harutake was born on {{ill|June 28|en|June 28|WD=Q2660|lt=June 28|1=June 28}}, {{ill|1827|en|1827|WD=Q7008|lt=1827|1=1827}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 10) to {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} and his consort Keijunin. At age six, he was granted the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank Upper|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and in {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839|1=1839}}, upon coming of age, he held the Rank of {{ill|Daizen Daibu|ja|大膳大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen Daibu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大膳職|1=Dazai Gon no Daibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438575}}. In {{ill|1842|en|1842|WD=Q7625|lt=1842|1=1842}}, he was appointed head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. During his career, Harutake also served as {{ill|Right Guard Officer|ja|右兵衛佐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Guard Officer|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵衛府|1=Hyōefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393702}} and reached the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, he resigned as Minister of Popular Affairs. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Harutake influenced the abolition of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}}, consolidating control over calendar and astronomical affairs under the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. Despite advocating for a revised {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, his proposals were delayed due to the new government's lack of interest. Harutake passed away at 43 in {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}. His posthumous name was Yangō-in-den Shōsanmi Abe Harutake-kyō, and his remains rest at {{ill|Baijinji Temple|ja|梅林寺 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baijinji Temple|1=Bairin-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q803940}}. == Succession == With the death of Harutake's son Harutsuna in {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, the family's leadership passed to his adopted son, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}. However, by {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}, the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} was disbanded by the new government. By {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}, the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} replaced the lunisolar system. == GenealogyTsuchimikado Haruyasu, "Tsuchimikado Family Records" (University of Tokyo Archives). == * Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Harunaga|ja|土御門晴親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Harunaga|1=Tsuchimikado Harechika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423479}} * Mother: Keijunin (consort) * Spouse: Daughter of {{ill|Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja|廣橋三成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Saneyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Children: *** Sons: Harutsuna *** Daughters: Masuko (married {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}}) * Adopted Son: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|ja|土御門晴榮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Haruyasu|1=Tsuchimikado Harenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17315120}} (son of {{ill|Nishikori Hisataka|ja|錦織久隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Hisataka|1=Q134509174|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134509174}}) == Notes == == References == {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|土御門晴雄|version=99794169|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11423480|ja|土御門晴雄}} [[Category:1827 births]] [[Category:1869 deaths]] [[Category:Court nobles of the Edo period]] [[Category:Eighty Eight Court Ministers]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] [[Category:People of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Tsuchimikado Family (Abe Clan)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
